refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall update the worksite model within the customer-required timeliness (p95 ≤ 1.0 s) for operational use, ensuring data accuracy and availability for all stakeholders.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is decomposed to explicitly address timeliness, data accuracy, and availability, which are critical for operational effectiveness and safety in a high-risk environment.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the 95th percentile latency of worksite model updates under representative operational scenarios; verify data accuracy through cross-validation with sensor inputs; confirm availability via system uptime and interface responsiveness tests.
sub_frs: FR1: Acquire and process telemetry data from vehicles and sensors within 0.5 s latency.
FR2: Update the centralized worksite model with processed data ensuring consistency and accuracy within 0.3 s latency.
FR3: Distribute updated worksite model data to all relevant modules and stakeholders within 0.2 s latency.

candidate_dps: DP1: Real-time telemetry data ingestion and preprocessing pipeline.
DP2: Centralized worksite model database with in-memory caching for low-latency updates.
DP3: Event-driven publish-subscribe distribution mechanism for worksite model updates.
selected_dp.selected_dp: Combination of DP1, DP2, and DP3 to meet the overall latency and accuracy requirements.

potential_sources: Data synchronization between telemetry ingestion and model update.
Latency dependencies between model update and distribution.
coupling_check.notes: DPs are logically decoupled with well-defined interfaces; event-driven architecture reduces tight coupling.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network link latency is stable and within acceptable bounds.
Risk of data loss or delay due to network congestion or hardware failure.
Assumes sufficient processing power in Vehicle and NGHE Solution modules.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Monitor network performance and consider fallback mechanisms for telemetry data loss.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Centralized processing and management of worksite model updates including telemetry ingestion, model update, and distribution.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives real-time telemetry data from Vehicle modules.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Supports data communication between NGHE Solution and other modules.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Sends updated worksite model data to Vehicle and Teleoperation modules.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Communicates safety-related updates to Safety Services.

module_containment_scores.notes: High containment of worksite update logic; low diffusion as updates are centralized.
Limited containment; mainly data source and consumer.
Receives updates but does not process or generate them.
Receives safety alerts but not involved in update processing.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Centralizing the worksite update logic in NGHE Solution minimizes diffusion and improves maintainability and timeliness.
containment_risks: Potential bottleneck in NGHE Solution if processing load spikes.
Single point of failure risk for worksite model updates.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Consider redundancy or load balancing within NGHE Solution to mitigate risks.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The refined FRs are well matched by the selected DPs with clear mapping and manageable coupling.

diffusion_risks: Centralization in NGHE Solution may increase risk of bottleneck and failure.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment scores support NGHE Solution as the best IM boundary for the DPs.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed via latency measurements and interface testing at the NGHE Solution boundary.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of FRs to NGHE Solution with well-defined external interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Add redundancy mechanisms in NGHE Solution IM to address bottleneck and failure risks.
Define fallback procedures for telemetry data loss.

decision.justification: The current level of decomposition and assignment meets the latency and safety criticality requirements with clear architectural boundaries and manageable risks.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Acquire and process telemetry data from vehicles and sensors within 0.5 s latency.
FR2: Update the centralized worksite model with processed data ensuring consistency and accuracy within 0.3 s latency.
FR3: Distribute updated worksite model data to all relevant modules and stakeholders within 0.2 s latency.

revise.why: No immediate revision needed; proceed to next decomposition level.

assumptions: Network link latency remains stable and within bounds.
Sufficient processing resources are available in NGHE Solution.
open_risks: Potential bottleneck and single point of failure in NGHE Solution.
Telemetry data loss due to network or hardware issues.
evidence_needed: Latency performance data under operational load.
Reliability metrics for NGHE Solution processing and network links.
